mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds +0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical edge over random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% during co-declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The concurrent payroll gain and unemployment drop fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with negative returns occurring in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal income during a housing market downturn signals eroding household balance sheets and reduced discretionary spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during housing downturns are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive edge relative to baseline.
